repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Problem: In autocmd APIs, a non-nil "pattern" containing only empty 'sub'-patterns is silently treated as nil, causing the fallback value to be unexpectedly used instead.

Solution: For `nvim_create_autocmd()`, raise a validation error (as no autocmds would be created).
For `nvim_{exec,clear}_autocmds()`, make it a no-op (as matching no autocmds is not an error).

Also fix `nvim_exec_autocmds()` docs describing its "pattern" default incorrectly. Add a test.

Fixes #37014. Technically breaking for anyone who (probably accidentally) relied on the old behaviour.
Originally I suggested making any empty 'sub'-pattern an error, but it feels too aggressive; `:autocmd` just skips those.

That said, not sure if a non-nil "pattern" with no non-empty 'sub'-patterns should be an error in all cases.
I've only made it so for `nvim_create_autocmd()` as described above. Thoughts?

comment_data.body: These test cases make it very clear, this definitely seems like an anti-feature.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1053,6 +1088,21 @@ describe('autocmd api', function()
 
       api.nvim_exec_autocmds('BufReadPre', { pattern = { 'foo', 'bar', 'baz', 'frederick' } })
       eq(22, api.nvim_get_var('autocmd_executed'))
+
+      eq('', api.nvim_buf_get_name(0))
+      api.nvim_exec_autocmds({ 'BufReadPre', 'BufReadPost' }, {})
+      eq(23, api.nvim_get_var('autocmd_executed'))
+
+      api.nvim_buf_set_name(0, 'bar')
+      api.nvim_exec_autocmds({ 'BufReadPre', 'BufReadPost' }, {})
+      eq(34, api.nvim_get_var('autocmd_executed'))
+
+      -- Non-nil, fully-empty patterns execute nothing.

comment_data.body: The default fallback to fname? #19113 describes the motivation for the change, but #19115 forgot to update the docs.

comment_data.body: I'd be happy to make it an error. Same could be extended to `event = {}` too for `nvim_exec_autocmds()` (currently a no-op), and `nvim_clear_autocmds()` (currently clears for all events, like `nil`; this PR also made it a no-op instead).

comment_regular_data.body:
> not sure if a non-nil "pattern" with no non-empty 'sub'-patterns should be an error in all cases.
> I've only made it so for `nvim_create_autocmd()` as described above. Thoughts?

The other cases being `nvim_exec_autocmds` and `nvim_clear_autocmds` ? As a followup we could make those error also, and just wait for feedback on master.

For this PR we can start with only `nvim_create_autocmd`

That's fine.

After thinking some more, I'm more undecided on whether it should be an error in all cases.
Likewise, for `event`s, `nvim_exec_autocmds({}, {})` is also a no-op, but `nvim_clear_autocmds({event={}})` clears for _all_ events (that might be another bug)... Sigh.

**Edit**: yeah, the `nvim_clear_autocmds()` thing is probably a bug. It even skips type checking on `event` entirely; `nvim_clear_autocmds({event=function() end})` is allowed and also treated like `nil`. I'll fix it... :rocket:

repo.description: [Contribution Fork] Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

title: fix(api)!: empty non-nil autocmd "pattern", "event", `nvim_exec_autocmds()` docs
